title: Feed-Forward Network
description: The broad transformer-block concept for the per-token layer that adds nonlinear processing after attention.
openingSummary: A feed-forward network is the part of a transformer block that works on each token state after attention, adding nonlinear processing so the model can reshape features instead of only passing context around.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A feed-forward network, often shortened to FFN, is the broad name for the per-token computation stage inside a transformer block. Attention gathers information from other positions first. The feed-forward network then takes each token state on its own, pushes it through learned projections and an activation, and returns an updated state to the residual stream. The same feed-forward design is reused at every token position, so it adds capacity without creating another token-to-token communication step.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Attention and feed-forward networks do different jobs. Attention decides which outside context each token should read. The feed-forward network turns that mixed context into sharper features, pattern detectors, and intermediate representations inside the token itself. Without this second stage, a transformer would move information between positions but would have much less room to transform that information into useful nonlinear features.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: Picture the word vector for "bank" after attention has read the rest of the sentence. Attention can bring in clues such as money or river. The feed-forward network is the next step that reshapes that updated vector so the model can emphasize the meaning that now fits the surrounding context. In a standard dense feed-forward network every token goes through the same hidden path. In a sparse mixture-of-experts design a router sends the token through only a few expert paths. Both are still feed-forward networks because they fill the same post-attention slot.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: A feed-forward network is a broad slot, not just one exact implementation. Standard dense FFNs, SwiGLU-style gated variants, and sparse mixture-of-experts layers are different ways to build the same stage. The feed-forward network is also not a replacement for attention: it does not decide which other tokens to read. It works after attention and transforms each token state separately once that context has already been gathered.
